title: Expects-response send flags and delivery annotation
description:
Implement --expects-response and --responds-to flags on h2 send. From docs/design-expects-response.md.

Sections: All (this doc is small and self-contained)

Key deliverables:
- --expects-response flag on h2 send: delivers message, then trigger_add on recipient daemon
- --responds-to flag on h2 send: send body first (if present), then trigger_remove on own daemon
- Trigger ID generation (8-char hex) with collision retry (one retry, then fatal)
- Exit code 2 for "delivered without tracking" partial success
- Target required when body present, optional for close-only
- Message.ExpectsResponse and Message.TriggerID fields
- Delivery annotation: (response expected, id: <id>) in deliver() format string
- Unit tests: trigger creation, annotation format, trigger_add failure, ID collision
  retry/fatal, responds-to with/without body, body-without-target error, missing socket

References:
- Plan: docs/design-expects-response.md (all sections)
- Depends on: h2-ifg.4 (CLI/socket protocol — trigger_add/remove via socket must exist)
